meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't scale projects (Advanced Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't scale projects. A Advanced-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve Scaling Issues
content.seo_description: Stop wasting time on boilerplate. Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit with Antigravity and Claude Code to scale your freelance projects and ship faster.
content.answer_block: To overcome scaling bottlenecks, stop building from scratch. Implement a modular SaaS starter kit that integrates authentication, database schemas, and billing as pre-configured blocks. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for automated implementation, you reduce development cycles by 80%, allowing you to focus on high-value client features.
content.intro:
The midnight oil is burning, another client deadline looms, and you’re still wrestling with user authentication boilerplate for the tenth time this year. You aren't scaling; you're just repeating yourself. Every hour spent configuring database connections or hooking up Stripe webhooks is an hour stolen from the high-level architecture that actually justifies your premium rates.

But the tide is turning. By shifting from manual coding to an automated, kit-based workflow, you transform from a reactive developer into a high-leverage architect. This isn't just about faster code—it's about building a repeatable machine that turns ideas into production-grade SaaS in hours, not weeks.

steps.title: Architect the Core Blueprint
steps.description: Define the essential infrastructure using Antigravity to ensure consistent database and auth patterns across all your future client projects.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular SaaS boilerplate architecture including Next.js 14, Supabase auth, and Stripe integration with clean separation of concerns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize project structure based on the Antigravity modular architecture schema'

steps.title: Automate Database Migrations
steps.description: Use AI to generate and execute migration scripts that maintain type safety between your frontend and the database.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Drizzle ORM migration script for a multi-tenant SaaS schema including users, organizations, and subscription tiers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --execute 'npx drizzle-kit generate && npx drizzle-kit push'

steps.title: Inject Auth Middleware
steps.description: Secure your routes instantly by deploying pre-verified authentication logic directly into your starter kit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement Next.js middleware to protect routes and verify session tokens against a Supabase backend.

steps.title: Configure Billing Pipelines
steps.description: Standardize your Stripe implementation so that every new project has a pre-built billing portal and webhook handler.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust Stripe webhook handler for subscription status updates and invoice generation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit app/api/webhooks/stripe/route.ts --apply 'Stripe event handling logic'

steps.title: Finalize CI/CD Pipelines
steps.description: Ensure your starter kit is production-ready by automating deployment configurations for Vercel or Railway.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, testing, and deployment to production on merge.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal --execute 'git add . && git commit -m "Finalize starter kit architecture"'

pro_tips: Always wrap your database calls in a custom repository pattern to make swapping backends trivial later.
Use Claude Code to document your own starter kit; treat your boilerplate as a living product, not just code.
Inject 'feature flags' into your starter kit early so you can toggle experimental client features without redeploying.
Maintain a separate 'private template' repository that you pull from, rather than just copying and pasting folders.
Standardize your error handling across all API routes to save hours of debugging in production.
content.next_steps: Clone your new repository, perform a dry-run deployment to a staging environment, and start mapping out your next client project requirements.
content.conversion_block: Stop building the same foundation over and over. Leverage the power of AI-assisted scaffolding to reclaim your time and scale your freelance agency. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
